title: OAuth error bodies leak refresh tokens and auth codes to end users
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access / Error Orchestration

Location: src/oauth.rs line 596 (send_token_request) and line 654 (create_api_key), plus src/claude_credentials.rs line 229 (refresh_and_load).

Violation: When a token exchange, refresh, or API-key creation fails, the full response body is interpolated directly into the error message via anyhow::bail! and the format! macro:
  - oauth.rs:596: bail!("Token exchange failed ({status}): {body}");
  - oauth.rs:654: bail!("API key creation failed ({status}): {body}");
  - claude_credentials.rs:229: return Err(format!("Token refresh failed ({status}): {body}"));
Anthropic's OAuth endpoint routinely echoes back the submitted grant_type, client_id, redirect_uri, scope, AND — critically — the authorization code or refresh_token as part of a validation error (e.g., "invalid_grant: refresh_token 'xxx...yyy' is expired"). This message then propagates upstream: the ProxyError::InvalidBody variant that swallows it ultimately lands in an axum Response body (proxy.rs line 297 "Token exchange failed: {e}") and goes to the end user's browser or CLI — meaning REFRESH TOKENS OR AUTH CODES CAN LEAK TO ANY CLIENT THAT TRIGGERED THE FAILURE. This is a critical credential-exfiltration path.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. In oauth.rs and claude_credentials.rs, replace the body interpolation with a dedicated sanitized error:
     - Extract only the short error code field (`error`, `error_description`) from a JSON parse attempt.
     - Log the full body at debug! level ONLY (tracing will not ship to the user).
     - Return an anyhow/String error containing ONLY status code + short error code.
  2. Add a test that injects a mock 400 response containing 'refresh_token_XXX' and asserts the returned Err string does NOT contain that substring.
  3. Similar fix for any other response.text().await.unwrap_or_default() on an auth path — grep for this pattern as a follow-up audit.
